_doc: Fixture for B10. The continuous-run scheduler had a bug where a sub-loop with a very short interval could starve longer-interval sub-loops by monopolising the fast clock. The pre-fix scheduler picked the next due loop greedily without resetting the priority queue between fast ticks. This fixture captures the planted starvation: loop 'fast' has interval_ms=10 and loop 'slow' has interval_ms=200, run over 1500ms of simulated time. A pre-fix scheduler runs 'fast' ~150 times and 'slow' 0 times. The post-fix scheduler MUST run 'slow' at least 6 times in the same window.
